artifacts.title: SS Yarmouth / SS Frederick Douglass
artifacts.caption: The first ship of the Black Star Line, purchased as the SS Yarmouth (1887 Nova Scotia build) and publicly renamed the SS Frederick Douglass in fall 1919. Photograph c. 1919–1922.

source.institution: T.H. Franklin / SSHSA collection, University of Baltimore Library (reproduced in Watson, Steamboat Bill)

artifacts.haystack: ss yarmouth / ss frederick douglass the first ship of the black star line, purchased as the ss yarmouth (1887 nova scotia build) and publicly renamed the ss frederick douglass in fall 1919. photograph c. 1919–1922. black star line ship yarmouth frederick douglass    t.h. franklin / sshsa collection, university of baltimore library (reproduced in watson, steamboat bill)

artifacts.title: UNIA Flag
artifacts.caption: The UNIA's red, black, and green tricolor, adopted at the 1920 International Convention. The flag remains in use by UNIA-ACL today and has become the pan-African flag worldwide.

source.institution: UNIA

artifacts.title: Negro World — Vol. VIII No. 24, July 31 1920
artifacts.caption: Front page of the UNIA's official newspaper, the Negro World, published the week before the August 1920 International Convention. Peak circulation reached 200,000 across English, Spanish, and French editions.

source.institution: NYPL Schomburg Center

artifacts.title: Marcus Garvey — Provisional President of Africa, 1920
artifacts.caption: Keystone View Co. stereoview portrait of Garvey in the regalia of his self-declared title Provisional President of Africa, proclaimed at the August 1920 UNIA Convention. The title was symbolic — a claim staked against colonial rule — not an office he ever held politically.

source.institution: Library of Congress — Keystone View Company

artifacts.haystack: marcus garvey — provisional president of africa, 1920 keystone view co. stereoview portrait of garvey in the regalia of his self-declared title provisional president of africa, proclaimed at the august 1920 unia convention. the title was symbolic — a claim staked against colonial rule — not an office he ever held politically. portrait provisional president regalia keystone 1920    library of congress — keystone view company

artifacts.title: SS Phyllis Wheatley Brochure
artifacts.caption: Black Star Line promotional brochure for the 'Phyllis Wheatley' — a ship Garvey advertised, sold shares for, but never actually owned. The phantom vessel is a central piece of the 1923 mail fraud case.

source.institution: Black Star Line / UNIA

artifacts.haystack: ss phyllis wheatley brochure black star line promotional brochure for the 'phyllis wheatley' — a ship garvey advertised, sold shares for, but never actually owned. the phantom vessel is a central piece of the 1923 mail fraud case. phyllis wheatley brochure fraud case black star line    black star line / unia

artifacts.title: Marcus Garvey with Amy Jacques Garvey, 1922
artifacts.caption: Garvey with Amy Jacques Garvey, his second wife, compiler and editor of the 1923 Philosophy and Opinions of Marcus Garvey, and the curator of his legacy for half a century after his 1940 death.

source.institution: Wikimedia Commons (original print digitization)

artifacts.haystack: marcus garvey with amy jacques garvey, 1922 garvey with amy jacques garvey, his second wife, compiler and editor of the 1923 philosophy and opinions of marcus garvey, and the curator of his legacy for half a century after his 1940 death. amy jacques portrait 1922 marriage   tag as amy jacques (2nd wife, editor of philosophy and opinions), not amy ashwood (1st wife, divorced 1922). wikimedia commons (original print digitization)
artifacts.validator_flag: Tag as Amy Jacques (2nd wife, editor of Philosophy and Opinions), NOT Amy Ashwood (1st wife, divorced 1922).

artifacts.title: Marcus Garvey, 1924
artifacts.caption: Seated portrait, photographed by the Bain News Service, August 5 1924 in New York. One of the canonical Bain portraits.

source.institution: Library of Congress — Bain Collection

artifacts.haystack: marcus garvey, 1924 seated portrait, photographed by the bain news service, august 5 1924 in new york. one of the canonical bain portraits. portrait bain 1924    library of congress — bain collection

artifacts.title: UNIA Experts Delegation to Liberia, 1924
artifacts.caption: The UNIA delegation of technical experts sent to Liberia in 1924, part of Garvey's plan to build a Black-governed homeland. The Liberian government reversed its approval of the UNIA settlement project before the delegation could establish itself — a decisive geopolitical defeat for the Back to Africa program.

source.institution: NYPL Schomburg Center

artifacts.haystack: unia experts delegation to liberia, 1924 the unia delegation of technical experts sent to liberia in 1924, part of garvey's plan to build a black-governed homeland. the liberian government reversed its approval of the unia settlement project before the delegation could establish itself — a decisive geopolitical defeat for the back to africa program. liberia delegation 1924 back to africa pan-african    nypl schomburg center

artifacts.title: Garvey, Schomburg, and mourners at John E. Bruce's grave, 1924
artifacts.caption: Mrs. John E. Bruce, Marcus Garvey, and Arthur Schomburg among mourners at the burial of John E. Bruce, Sr. — the Black journalist whose papers seeded the Schomburg Collection. Direct visual tie between Garvey and the institution that would become the archive of Black intellectual life.

source.institution: NYPL Schomburg Center — Photographs and Prints Division

artifacts.haystack: garvey, schomburg, and mourners at john e. bruce's grave, 1924 mrs. john e. bruce, marcus garvey, and arthur schomburg among mourners at the burial of john e. bruce, sr. — the black journalist whose papers seeded the schomburg collection. direct visual tie between garvey and the institution that would become the archive of black intellectual life. schomburg bruce 1924 funeral intellectual lineage   preserve mrs. bruce in any crop — her presence is the photograph's anchor, not garvey's. nypl schomburg center — photographs and prints division

artifacts.title: FBI File on Marcus Garvey — Section 1A
artifacts.caption: Bureau of Investigation file 190-1781-6, Section 1A (128 pages). Contains the foundational surveillance memos, the October 1921 Hoover letter, and the UNIA O Street Armory event flyer. The file number itself is a story — Garvey was investigated as a foreign agent years before he was prosecuted as a domestic criminal.

source.institution: Federal Bureau of Investigation / FBI Vault

artifacts.haystack: fbi file on marcus garvey — section 1a bureau of investigation file 190-1781-6, section 1a (128 pages). contains the foundational surveillance memos, the october 1921 hoover letter, and the unia o street armory event flyer. the file number itself is a story — garvey was investigated as a foreign agent years before he was prosecuted as a domestic criminal. fbi surveillance 1919 1921 hoover   frame as evidence of the surveillance state, not evidence against garvey. the 1927 deportation was the bureau's intended outcome from the start. federal bureau of investigation / fbi vault
artifacts.validator_flag: Frame as evidence OF the surveillance state, not evidence against Garvey. The 1927 deportation was the Bureau's intended outcome from the start.

artifacts.title: FBI File on Marcus Garvey — Section 1B
artifacts.caption: FBI surveillance file Section 1B (130 pages) — continues the early-period memos. James Wormley Jones, the Bureau's first Black agent, was hired specifically to infiltrate the UNIA. His reports are here.

source.institution: Federal Bureau of Investigation / FBI Vault

artifacts.haystack: fbi file on marcus garvey — section 1b fbi surveillance file section 1b (130 pages) — continues the early-period memos. james wormley jones, the bureau's first black agent, was hired specifically to infiltrate the unia. his reports are here. fbi surveillance jones infiltration    federal bureau of investigation / fbi vault

artifacts.title: Mail Fraud Indictment — US v. Garvey, Case C 33-688
artifacts.caption: The canonical indictment that sent Garvey to federal prison. Eight counts of mail fraud plus conspiracy. The case turned on a single letter to shareholder Benny Dancy advertising the 'Phyllis Wheatley' — a ship the BSL never actually owned. Garvey alone convicted; all three co-defendants acquitted.

source.institution: NARA / US District Court, Southern District of New York (RG 21)

artifacts.haystack: mail fraud indictment — us v. garvey, case c 33-688 the canonical indictment that sent garvey to federal prison. eight counts of mail fraud plus conspiracy. the case turned on a single letter to shareholder benny dancy advertising the 'phyllis wheatley' — a ship the bsl never actually owned. garvey alone convicted; all three co-defendants acquitted. nara 1922 mail fraud prosecution trial phyllis wheatley dancy   the mail fraud conviction is widely treated as political prosecution. biden pardoned garvey posthumously in january 2025. nara / us district court, southern district of new york (rg 21)
artifacts.validator_flag: The mail fraud conviction is widely treated as political prosecution. Biden pardoned Garvey posthumously in January 2025.

artifacts.title: W.E.B. Du Bois — 'The Black Star Line' (Crisis, September 1922)
artifacts.caption: Du Bois's real-time public critique of the Black Star Line's collapse, published in The Crisis. Details the failures of each BSL ship, names the phantom Phyllis Wheatley, and delivers the famous line: 'Here then is the collapse of the only thing in the Garvey movement which was original or promising.' The primary document of the Du Bois–Garvey feud.

source.institution: The Crisis magazine / NAACP

artifacts.haystack: w.e.b. du bois — 'the black star line' (crisis, september 1922) du bois's real-time public critique of the black star line's collapse, published in the crisis. details the failures of each bsl ship, names the phantom phyllis wheatley, and delivers the famous line: 'here then is the collapse of the only thing in the garvey movement which was original or promising.' the primary document of the du bois–garvey feud. du bois crisis 1922 black star line critique feud   source conflict with garvey's own writings. surface the conflict — do not silently resolve the du bois vs garvey argument. both must coexist in the archive. the crisis magazine / naacp
artifacts.validator_flag: Source CONFLICT with Garvey's own writings. Surface the conflict — do not silently resolve the Du Bois vs Garvey argument. Both must coexist in the archive.

artifacts.title: Income Tax Indictment — US v. Garvey, Case C 38-771
artifacts.caption: The SECOND prosecution against Garvey, separate from the mail fraud case. Filed 1923, Nolle Prosequi entered June 9 1932. Alleged false income tax return for 1921 (reported $4,000, actual $10,000) plus perjury. Contains Amy Jacques Garvey's affidavit requesting bail cancellation while Garvey was at Atlanta Federal Penitentiary.

source.institution: NARA / US District Court, Southern District of New York (RG 21)

artifacts.haystack: income tax indictment — us v. garvey, case c 38-771 the second prosecution against garvey, separate from the mail fraud case. filed 1923, nolle prosequi entered june 9 1932. alleged false income tax return for 1921 (reported $4,000, actual $10,000) plus perjury. contains amy jacques garvey's affidavit requesting bail cancellation while garvey was at atlanta federal penitentiary. nara 1923 tax evasion prosecution amy jacques    nara / us district court, southern district of new york (rg 21)

artifacts.title: Explanation of the Objects of the UNIA (1921 phonograph)
artifacts.caption: Marcus Garvey's voice, recorded on phonograph in New York, July 1921. An abridged version (~3:44) of 'A Membership Appeal from Marcus Garvey to the Negro Citizens of New York.' This is one of only two surviving recordings of Garvey's voice.

source.institution: Internet Archive (community upload) / Wikimedia Commons

artifacts.haystack: explanation of the objects of the unia (1921 phonograph) marcus garvey's voice, recorded on phonograph in new york, july 1921. an abridged version (~3:44) of 'a membership appeal from marcus garvey to the negro citizens of new york.' this is one of only two surviving recordings of garvey's voice. audio 1921 garvey's voice phonograph a-side unia    internet archive (community upload) / wikimedia commons

artifacts.title: Hon. Marcus Garvey on His Return to the U.S.A. (1921)
artifacts.caption: B-side of the same July 1921 recording session. Garvey addresses federal efforts to deny him re-entry to the United States after his Caribbean and Central America tour. The second of only two surviving recordings of Garvey's voice in existence — no Jamaica-era recordings, no BBC recordings, no UNIA band masters.

source.institution: UCLA Marcus Garvey and UNIA Papers Project (Robert A. Hill)

artifacts.haystack: hon. marcus garvey on his return to the u.s.a. (1921) b-side of the same july 1921 recording session. garvey addresses federal efforts to deny him re-entry to the united states after his caribbean and central america tour. the second of only two surviving recordings of garvey's voice in existence — no jamaica-era recordings, no bbc recordings, no unia band masters. audio 1921 garvey's voice phonograph b-side surveillance  across ucla mgpp, loc, american radioworks, and wikimedia: these two sides are the only known recordings of garvey's voice. that scarcity is part of the story.  ucla marcus garvey and unia papers project (robert a. hill)
artifacts.scarcity_note: Across UCLA MGPP, LoC, American RadioWorks, and Wikimedia: these two sides are the ONLY known recordings of Garvey's voice. That scarcity is part of the story.
